item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stop committing criminal activities in my name, I just want to read the news! -2662

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is how you write humanoids. Just all-around brilliant. Great concept, great execution. great logs, great everything. Just the right amount of funny to tickle my soul without breaking the suspension of disbelief and the general sense of seriousness of the main site. And this:

"I'm only, like, two hundred years old! I'm barely legal!"

I laughed. This article warms me inside despite the fact it's about fanatical blood/death/sex cults trying to worship their god. If that's not skilled writing, I don't know what is.
Enjoy your 1166th upvote buddy. I'd upvote again if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine playing Titanfall 2 and your teammate makes a sick ass Kraber shot and over VC you hear the most horrible yet joyous eldritch screech that strikes fear and dread into the hearts of all who hear it

item.title: I agree
item.content:encoded: This article just seems like a joke to me, I don't see why it is in the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to let you know this article is hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He needs more gaming money. And probably some therapy at this point.

This is one of my favorites skips, and one I come back to when I need to unwind. It's the perfect balance of creepy and irreverent. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I propose somebody should increase his game budget. 50 USD ain't much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A well-deserved millennium milestone for one of the conceptually strongest characters on the site

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's No Way My Totally Unwilling Eldritch God Can Be This Cute!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God I love this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation can literally reset the entire reality but can't afford to spend more than $50 a month on video games to keep a brainwashing Eldritch God entertained smh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1: this guy needs a hug.
2: I feel like, when he finally decides to "settle down," he'll just make his containment team his cult and his only "godly demands" or whatever will straight up just be "keep up the good work keeping those blood cult freaks away from me, fellas."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. How it interacts with followers gives us a great read too, like a mix of comedy and confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel bad for him. He does not want all of this happen. Be just want a hug or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written, well formatted, interesting, and funny enough without being a joke SCP. Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor guy can't catch a break.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know the feeling. While I will admit that Steam is my go-to for PC-originals that it’s prohibitively difficult to legitimately get your hands on a console port of & PC-exclusives, I do hate it when the vents don’t work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, sentient things are euclid by default.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's so done. It's great +1

item.title: My favourite
item.content:encoded:
Honestly this is my favorite SCP, a grumpy teenager SCP who wants to be left alone.

It also ties back into how the Foundation is cold not cruel, the SCP himself doesn't cause any trouble and is perfectly cooperative with the Foundation and is thus afforded more freedom.

Hopefully one day he's allowed to just live quietly with no freaky sex cults popping up in his name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny enough, there are gods in the D&D campaigns I build that are based on 2662. The young god's reactions to these situations are so genuine, too!

It's always a fun read and gets a laugh out of me every time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like, definitely my favorite of all of them. I just love this concept. An elder god that just wants to be left alone. Just a regular dude that everyone tries to worship just because he has tentacles. He seems really nice too. Please let me know if you make any tales or more info about him, I really really like this dude. And hey, if the foundation treats him well and like a friend… they might just have a powerful ally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. It's not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is missing a -j at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I'm only, like, 200 years old! I'm barely legal!"

+1

item.title: HEY
item.content:encoded: It's like teenage Cthulu!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lovely

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of some uncontainably-powerful entity choosing to remain a guest of the Foundation for its own purposes. That and this being very well written means you get the +1, for a +600 total.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is easily one if my favorite Skips. Great job!

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Alright that's pretty hilarious. 😂

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LOL poor 2662.
If Cthulhu says you need to read the Bible, you've got problems. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am 100% on board with this canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a chill dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i…am very confused how this isnt a SCP-J, but im ok with it
well done

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This is hilarious, I hope you enjoy the audio version!

https://youtu.be/ns_6LaegFgY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just realized this is 100x funnier if you imagine 2662 having Morty's voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like this article. It's well written, it's clever and funny, and yet still has some very powerful underlying horror in the fact that these things just sort of keep happening, but I feel required to downvote for a very specific reason.

<22:51> Five injured civilians climb out of the hole left by the detonation. SCP-2662 leaves the shower to investigate the noise.

After further investigation, all involved personnel were civilians posing as personnel. It is unknown as to the whereabouts of the actual researcher and security officers.

Task Force Tau-9 arrive at the off-site meeting point only to be told no-such mission exists. Task Force Tau-9 begin moving back to Site ██.

This is just completely unbelievable. It would be entirely fine as a joke, but…even with supernatural timing and planning, this degree of civilian infiltration of the Foundation, and the Foundation's forces themselves getting called away? Just…just no, man. Especially not with the aforementioned

SCP-2662 is unable to affect humans that have a 2 or higher MARS (Mind-Affecting Resistance Scale) score. [SCP-2662 can only affect approximately 5 percent of the human population and 2 percent of Foundation personnel. It is therefore considered a low-risk cognitohazard.]

Somebody who can ORDER A MOBILE TASK FORCE AWAY in the Foundation is that susceptible? Just…just so much no. :/

That said, again, it's really well written, and has just so much to offer. So many great little bits to pick up from it.

<22:56> After a brief struggle, Agents ███ and █████ are pinned down and tied with rope by the civilians. The injured civilians approach SCP-2662. The uninjured civilians stab their hands with knives, masturbate, and then draw unknown symbols on the walls with a mixture of sexual fluids and blood.

<22:58> SCP-2662: "Aw man, come on. That's really not sanitary. Or necessary." SCP-2662 appears to gag upon witnessing the symbols. The injured civilians successfully corner SCP-2662 in the shower stall.　

Supernaturally caused premature ejaculation? Just damn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god yes this is awesome.

+1 Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An eldrith abomination telling a psychotic researcher that she needs Jesus.

I never noticed this. This makes the article even awesomer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2662: "I- what? Look, can you go read a bible or something? You really, really need it."

An eldrith abomination telling a psychotic researcher that she needs Jesus.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did write Skeeve Online about 2662 (and a few other SCPs besides). It can be uncomfortable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a tale about this guy? There should be. I also wonder if he'll be this sane when he fully matures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not-So-Old One. Oh God, my sides.

…of course, when he grows up, things shall be less funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really following the usual SCP clinical tone here and not really going into much necessary detail like its origins. Still really fun though. Personally I think this goes in the Joke tab and needs to be expanded. Not up voting for previously stated reasons but not down voting because it is still really funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like a joke at first glance, but it is not when you found out this is REALLY the elder one growing up. Besides, this is just hilarious! Creepy but hilarious! Upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah. Being worshipped when not asked to be worshipped. I know how it feels XD

Edit: after realising that I'm scrub in university, I take this back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful.+1. 10/10.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love SCP-2662's reaction and quotes when those cult members are trying to impress him.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Living things are Euclid by default.

item.title: Orgy's & Oh My.....
item.content:encoded:
Didn't think this would be good… Until I read the logs.
I'm still slapping myself to wake up from the laughing due to the Orgy bits.
+1

item.title: Re: Amazing.
item.content:encoded:
Poor, poor eldritch abomination.

This is one of those few SCP's that I'd like to actually meet.

item.title: Amazing.
item.content:encoded:
Words cannot describe how much I love this.

Poor Cthulhu. He was probably just outta R'lyeh, studying abroad.
FROTHING AT THE MOUTH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this +1

It is unknown as to the whereabouts of the actual researcher and security officers.

This should read: "The whereabouts of the actual researcher and security officers remain unknown." You might also consider replacing "actual" with "real", which is more appropriate in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reason I downvoted this is that a mainlist skip can be funny, but that humor needs to be incidental. The dialogue is so tongue-in-cheek and self aware that I can't look at it as such. Otherwise, this is a great joke, I enjoyed it, but it doesn't actually belong here.

For reference, compare to the dialogue in Humans Go Home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor bastard

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Jesus Christ! Stop! Please! I just took a shower!"

upvoted for poor teen cthulhu who can't make these friggin cults stop messing him up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is an interesting alternate interpretation. The implications of that are great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My Headcanon is that this isn't an unintentional thing due to 2662's own nature, but rather due to extreme overzealousness on the part of 2662's parents/progenitors who are arranging blind dates. Why? Cuz they want grandentities, that's why. And they disapprove of his relationship with the Foundation, because clearly that relationship isn't going anywhere fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For me, this sort of thing seems like it should be object classed Safe Keter instead of Euclid Keter. Just seems like it'd fit better since without the constant cult attempts to get to him, Teenthulhu doesn't seem to be dangerous on his own. I definitely agree that the violent/sexual cult things push him to Keter, but it seems better to have him originally classified as Safe.

Other than that, I LOVED this one. It's just like The Clown Vaccine (2170), it's creepy as hell while still being absurdly funny.

+1, and may Lovecraft have mercy on your soul.
